message:
test(fcp-conformance/streaming-websocket): pin WsMessage + WsCloseFrame + WsConfig contract (resolves flywheel_connectors-f98hy)

32 tests pinning the WebSocket primitives every push connector
composes alongside SSE. Zero prior conformance coverage.

- MAX_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_SIZE = 16 MiB hard ceiling pinned as a public
  const (DoS protection regardless of caller config)
- WsMessage 5 RFC 6455 variants (Text/Binary/Ping/Pong/Close) with
  matching constructors text/binary/ping/pong producing the right
  variant
- Predicate fan-out: is_text/is_binary/is_close are exclusive — Ping
  and Pong are NEITHER text nor binary (control frames are NOT data
  frames); is_close handles both Close(Some) and Close(None)
- as_text/as_binary return None for every non-matching variant
  (Ping payload bytes MUST NOT be exposed via as_binary)
- json deserialization: Text→from_str, Binary→from_slice,
  Ping/Pong/Close→error, malformed Text propagates serde_json::Error
- WsCloseFrame::normal = (1000, "Normal closure") per RFC 6455;
  going_away = (1001, "Going away"); new preserves both fields;
  PartialEq compares full struct
- WsConfig::default 8 documented values (connect_timeout=30s,
  ping_interval=Some(30s), pong_timeout=10s, max_message_size=16 MiB,
  empty headers, auto_reconnect=true, max_reconnect=Some(10),
  reconnect_delay=1s); new()=default()
- Builder chain preservation + with_header replace-on-duplicate-key
- Debug redacts header VALUES with [REDACTED] (Bearer tokens MUST
  NOT leak via Debug — same security contract as SseConfig)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
